questions.q: What was the main task of the Constituent Assembly of India?
options: To draft the Constitution of India
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was formed to draft the Constitution of India.

questions.q: On what date did the Constituent Assembly hold its first sitting?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly held its first sitting on 9 December 1946.

questions.q: Who served as the President of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the President of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Who chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: In which year was the Constituent Assembly of India constituted?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was constituted in 1946.

questions.q: Roughly how long did the Constituent Assembly take to complete the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Assembly took nearly three years to draft and finalise the Constitution.

questions.q: On which date did the Constituent Assembly adopt the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution on 26 November 1949.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly was set up under a plan associated with which mission of 1946?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was set up under the plan of the Cabinet Mission of 1946.

questions.q: After independence, the Constituent Assembly also functioned as the country's first:
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly also acted as the provisional Parliament of India until elections were held.

questions.q: The members of the Constituent Assembly were chiefly chosen through which method?
options: Indirect election by the provincial legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: Members were largely chosen through indirect election by the members of the provincial legislative assemblies.

questions.q: Who served as the temporary or provisional chairman at the first sitting of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha was the provisional chairman at the Assembly's first sitting on 9 December 1946.

questions.q: Who moved the historic Objectives Resolution in the Constituent Assembly in December 1946?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru moved the Objectives Resolution, which laid down the ideals later reflected in the Preamble.

questions.q: Who served as the Constitutional Adviser to the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Sir B.N. Rau served as the Constitutional Adviser and prepared the initial draft of the Constitution.

questions.q: How many members did the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly have?
questions.explanation: The Drafting Committee had seven members and was chaired by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

questions.q: Who served as the Secretary to the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: H.V.R. Iyengar served as the Secretary of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Following the events of 1947, the Constituent Assembly of India was reconstituted to represent which territory?
options: The whole of undivided British India
The Dominion of India after partition
questions.explanation: After partition in 1947, the Assembly was reconstituted to frame the Constitution for the Dominion of India.

questions.q: Who was elected the Vice-President (Deputy Chairman) of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: H.C. Mukherjee was elected Vice-President of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Which committee of the Constituent Assembly was chaired by Jawaharlal Nehru?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru chaired the Union Powers Committee of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Which committee dealing with provincial matters was chaired by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel?
questions.explanation: Sardar Patel chaired the Provincial Constitution Committee, as well as the committee on fundamental rights and minorities.

questions.q: The national flag of independent India was formally adopted by the Constituent Assembly in which year?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the national flag on 22 July 1947.

questions.q: Approximately how many sessions did the Constituent Assembly hold while framing the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly held about eleven sessions over the course of nearly three years.

questions.q: On 24 January 1950 the Constituent Assembly also adopted which national symbols?
options: The national anthem and national song
questions.explanation: On 24 January 1950 the Assembly adopted Jana Gana Mana as the national anthem and Vande Mataram as the national song.

questions.q: Approximately how many members did the Constituent Assembly have at its first sitting in December 1946?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was envisaged with about 389 members before partition reduced its strength.

questions.q: Roughly how long, in years, months and days, did the Constituent Assembly take to frame the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Assembly took about 2 years, 11 months and 18 days to complete the Constitution.

questions.q: On 24 January 1950 the members of the Constituent Assembly also elected which person to a high constitutional office?
questions.explanation: On 24 January 1950 the Assembly elected Dr. Rajendra Prasad as the first President of India.

questions.q: Which woman member of the Constituent Assembly led the singing of Vande Mataram and Jana Gana Mana at its sessions?
questions.explanation: Sucheta Kripalani, a member of the Assembly, led the singing of Vande Mataram and Jana Gana Mana at its sessions, including the concluding session.

questions.q: Which member, known as one of the constitutional experts of the Assembly, was a member of the Drafting Committee from the Madras region?
questions.explanation: Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar, a noted jurist, was a member of the seven-member Drafting Committee.

questions.q: Which member of the Drafting Committee was also a prominent writer and later a Union minister, associated with drafting work?
questions.explanation: K.M. Munshi was a member of the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: After the reorganisation following partition, the effective strength of the Constituent Assembly was reduced to about how many members?
questions.explanation: After partition the Constituent Assembly's strength was reduced to about 299 members.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly held its final sitting and the new Parliament took over its functions in which year?
questions.explanation: The Assembly continued as the provisional Parliament until the first elected Parliament was formed in 1952.
